inclusion_criteria: Adults (≥18 years) with BMI ≥27.5 kg/m² enrolled in a weight management intervention.
Synchronous (real-time), remotely delivered, interactive weight management interventions (e.g., live phone/video consultations, live group sessions, real-time chat).
Reports barriers and/or facilitators to engagement with the remote intervention, explicitly or amenable to mapping to capability, opportunity, and motivation (COM-B) domains.
Primary empirical studies (qualitative, quantitative, or mixed-methods) that measure or describe engagement-related barriers/facilitators.
exclusion_criteria: Participants <18 years, BMI <27.5 kg/m², or populations not stratifiable to meet these thresholds.
Interventions delivered only face-to-face, asynchronous-only or fully self-guided digital programs without real-time interaction, or interventions not focused on weight management.
Studies that do not assess or describe barriers/facilitators to engagement (e.g., only report efficacy outcomes or demographic predictors without engagement constructs).
Non-primary research (systematic/scoping reviews, meta-analyses), protocols, editorials/opinions, conference abstracts, or case reports without primary engagement data.
screening_criteria.description: Screen studies for adult populations with excess weight (BMI ≥27.5) receiving synchronous, remote weight management interventions that report barriers/facilitators to engagement (COM-B), including primary qualitative, quantitative, or mixed-methods designs.

included_articles.title: Feasibility and acceptability of a rural, pragmatic, telemedicine-delivered healthy lifestyle programme.
Feasibility and acceptability of a technology-based, rural weight management intervention in older adults with obesity.
